ICD-10-CM Classification System

The International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) is the standard diagnostic tool for epidemiology, health management, and clinical purposes in the United States. The CDC is responsible for maintaining the ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes used by physicians, other providers, and insurers.

Structure of ICD-10-CM Codes

ICD-10-CM codes consist of 3 to 7 characters. The first character is always alphabetic. The second and third characters are numeric. The fourth through seventh characters may be either numeric or alphabetic.

Common Code Examples

  • E11.9 — Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications
  • I10 — Essential (primary) hypertension
  • J18.9 — Pneumonia, unspecified organism
  • Z87.891 — Personal history of other specified conditions

Annual Updates

The ICD-10-CM code set is updated annually. The October 1 effective date applies to all facilities. Healthcare providers must use the codes in effect on the date of service. LOINC and SNOMED CT are complementary clinical terminologies used alongside ICD-10-CM.
